title: Counts & Repeat
intro: Prefix any motion or operator with a number to repeat it: `3w` jumps three words, `2dd` deletes two lines, `d2w` deletes two words. The `.` command repeats your last change.

exercises.instruction: Jump three words forward to `four` with `3w`.
hints: Type the count first, then the motion: `3` `w`.

exercises.instruction: Delete both middle lines at once with `2dd`.

goal.buffer:
const keep1 = true;
const keep2 = true;

hints: `2dd` = delete two lines starting from the cursor.

exercises.instruction: There are two extra `e`s. The cursor is on the first one — delete both at once with `2x`.
hints: `2x` deletes two characters starting at the cursor.

exercises.instruction: Delete the first two words in one command with `d2w`.
Delete the word `bad` with `dw`, then press `.` twice to repeat the change until only `good code` remains.
hints: `.` repeats your last change — `dw` once, then `.` `.`

exercises.initialCode: one two three four five six

exercises.instruction: Delete the first three words with one command: `d3w`.
hints: The count can sit inside the operator: `d` `3` `w`.

exercises.instruction: Each line ends with a doubled semicolon. Delete one with `x`, move down with `j`, then repeat the change with `.`.
hints: `x`, `j`, `.` — the dot replays the delete on the second line.
